item.title: Shopify Analyst Who Saw Selloff Coming Says It’s Too Soon to Buy
item.description: The analyst who slapped the only sell rating on Shopify Inc. over valuation concerns ahead of its blistering rout said it’s still too soon to jump back in.

item.title: ‘Golden’ or ‘Garbage’: Milken Signals New Era for Private Credit
item.description: Months before Jamie Dimon’s infamous remark about more than one “cockroach” lurking in credit markets, TCW Group Inc. Chief Executive Officer Katie Koch warned the direct lending industry was due for a reckoning.

item.title: Nvidia, Microsoft, AWS Expanding Classified Military AI Use
item.description: The Pentagon has struck agreements with four more technology companies for expansive use of advanced artificial intelligence tools on classified military networks, according to a Defense Department statement and two defense officials briefed on the matter.

item.title: Meta’s AI Spending Overshadows Addiction Lawsuits, Youth Bans
item.description: Social media companies face backlash over safety of youth on their platforms

item.title: War Forces G-7 Officials to Pencil in Possible Rate Hikes
item.description: If Warsh pushes for a US rate cut, it’s likely to go against the grain of G-7 action as soon as June.

item.title: American Farmers Shun USDA Surveys as Trust in Data Erodes
item.description: American farmers are ignoring US Department of Agriculture surveys in the latest sign of distrust in government data.

item.title: Bond Traders Brace for New Gilts Selloff as Local Elections Loom
item.description: UK bond investors are shifting their focus from monetary policy to politics, with local elections next week seen as a potential catalyst for turmoil in government that could trigger a renewed selloff.

item.title: Nebius Agrees to Buy Startup That Makes AI Run Faster, Cheaper
item.description: The cloud provider’s deal for Eigen AI will help the inference business

item.title: Exxon Profit Surprises Analysts Despite Iran War’s Tumult
item.description: Exxon Mobil Corp. outperformed expectations after oil-production increases from Guyana and the Permian Basin helped offset supply losses due to the Middle East war.

item.title: Japan Can Boost the Yen But That’s Only a Short-Term Fix
item.description: Analysts doubt that intervention can do more than briefly stabilize markets.

item.title: Wall Street Is Bullish on Oracle, But Investors Aren’t Buying It
item.description: To Wall Street, Oracle Corp. is a screaming buy. To many investors, however, the company’s relationship with OpenAI, its sizable debt load and the durability of its software business have made owning the stock a risk.

item.title: Bolivia apuesta por el turismo para reactivar su economía y atraer divisas
item.description: Conocida desde hace tiempo por la minería y los combustibles fósiles, esta nación andina apuesta ahora por sus recursos naturales como forma de atraer a visitantes extranjeros —y divisas fuertes—.

item.title: Chevron Profit Beats Estimates on War-Driven Surge in Oil Prices
item.description: Chevron Corp. exceeded profit expectations as higher oil and natural gas prices, as well as supplies from the acquisition of Hess Corp., outweighed production outages from the Iran war.

item.title: Bolivia Seeks a $3 Billion Tourism Boost to Revive Its Crumbling Economy
item.description: Long known for mining and fossil fuels, the Andean nation is now eyeing its natural resources as a way to attract foreign visitors — and hard currency.

item.title: Ares Earnings Miss Estimates Despite Record Fundraising Haul
item.description: Ares Management Corp., facing a slump in dealmaking while navigating broader market turmoil, reported first-quarter earnings that missed Wall Street estimates even as wealthy clients helped fuel a record $30 billion of fundraising during the period.

item.title: Uber Acquires Rival FlyTaxi in Hong Kong Push, Sing Tao Reports
item.description: Uber Technologies Inc. has agreed to acquire Hong Kong’s FlyTaxi as part of its expansion plans in the city, Sing Tao Daily reported, citing an announcement by the US-based company.

item.title: Emerging Markets Eke Out Gains in Thin-Volume Holiday Trading
item.description: Emerging-market benchmarks for stocks and currencies posted small gains in holiday-thinned trade, with most markets in Asia, Europe and Africa closed for Labour Day.

item.title: UK Billionaire’s Family Office Targets More Private Equity Exits
item.description: The billionaire UK founder of a home-repair business acquired by Brookfield Asset Management is working to ramp up his family office’s private equity exits, as larger buyout firms grapple with a dry spell for transactions.

item.title: AI Agents Are Becoming Day Traders, But Gains Are Elusive
item.description: Jake Nesler’s AI trading bot got one big decision right in its first week. It ignored the chase.

item.title: BOE Considers Keeping Digital Pound On Ice as Rivals Race Ahead
item.description: Much has changed since the UK Treasury and Bank of England said a digital pound was “likely” to be needed, just three years ago.

item.title: Japan Likely Spent About $34.5 Billion on Yen Intervention
item.description: Japan likely spent around $34.5 billion Thursday in its first currency intervention to prop up the yen since July 2024, according to a Bloomberg analysis of central bank accounts.

item.title: Hungary Releases Terms of €1 Billion Orban-Era China Loan
item.description: Hungary has released previously undisclosed details of a €1 billion ($1.2 billion) loan that outgoing Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s government took from China in 2024, news website 444.hu reported Friday.

item.title: Energy Prices Underpin Strong Outlook for Japan’s Trading Houses
item.description: Japan’s big five trading houses are set to benefit from war-related supply disruptions this year, with higher energy and metals prices driving stronger earnings.

item.title: How Taiwan Became the World's Most Perilous Geopolitical Chokepoint
item.description: Eyck Freymann on the biggest flashpoint of them all.

item.title: Odd Lots: How Taiwan Could Become a Global Chokepoint (Podcast)
item.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has highlighted the potential for long-running theoretical chokepoints to turn into reality, with dramatic results for both geopolitics and the global economy. But the hypothetical scenario that policymakers have arguably been losing the most sleep over for decades is the prospect of a major conflict between China and Taiwan. So how likely is it, and what would such a conflict actually look like? On this episode, we speak with Eyck Freymann, author of the new

item.title: ECB’s Kocher Says Interest-Rate Hold Gives Time to Assess Risks
item.description: The European Central Bank’s decision to hold interest rate this week provides policymakers with more time to assess whether the Middle East crisis triggers prolonged inflation, according to Governing Council member Martin Kocher.

item.title: Warm, Wild Weather Puts Europe on Storm and Drought Watch in May
item.description: Europe is set for weeks of erratic and increasingly warm weather over the coming month, raising the risk of storms, early season drought stress and increasing cooling demand.

item.title: Nagel Favors ECB June Hike Unless Outlook Improves Markedly
item.description: The European Central Bank will need to increase interest rates in June if there isn’t a significant change in the outlook, according to Bundesbank President Joachim Nagel.

item.title: FTSE 100 Drops in Thin Holiday Trading as NatWest Results Weigh
item.description: UK shares dropped in thin holiday trading as NatWest Group Plc fell more than 3% after reporting net interest income that came in slightly below expectations.

item.title: Japan Reveals First Loans to US Projects Under Trade Deal
item.description: Japan’s government-backed lender and private bankers announced their first loans as part of the nation’s $550 billion commitment to US projects under a trade agreement with President Donald Trump.
